statement: a, b, c are positive real numbers
Cauchy-Schwarz inequality (Engel/Titu form): for positive reals x_i, y_i, sum(x_i^2 / y_i) >= (sum x_i)^2 / (sum y_i)
AM-GM inequality: for positive reals x, y, x + y >= 2*sqrt(x*y), equivalently sqrt(x*y) <= (x+y)/2
Cauchy-Schwarz in the form: (sum_i u_i * v_i)^2 <= (sum_i u_i^2)(sum_i v_i^2)
Prove that a^2/sqrt(3a^2 + 8b^2 + 14ab) + b^2/sqrt(3b^2 + 8c^2 + 14bc) + c^2/sqrt(3c^2 + 8a^2 + 14ca) >= (a+b+c)/5 for all positive reals a, b, c
